title: Polytopic Quantum Resource Theories: Geometry and Structures
authors: Moein Naseri, Chirag Srivastava, Chandan Datta, John H. Selby, Shubhayan Sarkar
abstract: Quantum resource theories provide a unifying framework to quantify, compare, and manipulate quantum resources under well-defined operational constraints. Here, we consider any resource theory where the set of free states can be expressed as a convex combination of a set of quantum states, referred to as extremal states and name them as polytopic quantum resource theories (PQRT). These include some of the most studied resource theories, such as coherence and magic. We formulate a novel tensorial representation of PQRTs that reveals the underlying geometry of these theories and provides insight into the origin of the resources. We further address a fundamental question in resource theories that when two theories should be regarded as physically equivalent, and to this purpose we introduce notions of homomorphism and isomorphism that compare both the structure of free states and the allowed transformations. Using the tools we develop, we find results revealing the geometrical and structural foundations of such theories. Interestingly, we find that all polytopic resource theories with a fixed number of pure extremal points are equivalent under a physical map, up to normalisation. Additionally, we introduce linearly independent polytopic resource theories (resource theory of ``basis-non-convexity''), where the set of extremal free states forms a basis of the quantum density operators. We further study the categorical structures of PQRTs beyond single systems.
summary: This paper introduces Polytopic Quantum Resource Theories (PQRTs) as a geometric framework to unify the study of quantum resources. It develops sophisticated mathematical tools, including homomorphisms and isomorphisms, to rigorously compare different resource theories. The work reveals deep structural equivalences between these theories and provides monotonicity properties for various resource measures.
main_problem: The paper develops a general, unified framework to quantify, compare, and manipulate various quantum resources (like entanglement or coherence) under operational constraints.
main_result: It establishes that all Polytopic Quantum Resource Theories (PQRTs) sharing a fixed number of pure extremal states are equivalent under a physical map, and introduces rigorous mathematical tools (homomorphism/isomorphism) to compare their structures.
method: The authors use convex geometry, linear algebra, and tensor representations to model the set of free states as a polytope, allowing for the formulation of equivalence criteria via homomorphisms and isomorphisms.
model_or_system: The theory applies to general quantum systems described by finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, where the resource is defined by the convex hull of a finite set of extremal pure states.
key_observables: Resource measures such as Robustness ($MR$), Geometric Measure ($MF$), and Negativity ($MN$) are analyzed for their monotonicity under free operations.
important_parameters: The number of pure extremal states ($|S_{	ext{ver}}|$), the dimension of the Hilbert space ($d$), and the structure of the underlying basis are key parameters.
main_assumptions: The analysis often assumes finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces and relies on the ability to characterize free operations using Semidefinite Programming (SDP) feasibility problems.
figures_summary: Not specified, but the notes detail the mathematical structure of the equivalence proofs, involving tensor representations and mapping diagrams.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first defining PQRTs geometrically, then introducing tensorial representations, followed by developing formal notions of homomorphism and isomorphism to compare theories, and finally analyzing the monotonicity of various resource measures.
why_interesting: This work provides a deep mathematical structure to resource theories, which is fundamental for understanding the limits of quantum information processing and resource quantification in any quantum physical system.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
